summary: Matched four item source files, primarily replacing m2c/placeholder stubs with C implementations for Lucky, Maril, Octarock, and Whispy Apple item behavior. The PR also added item-specific attribute and item-var structs in itCommonItems.h, tightened several item function prototypes, and corrected shared collision helper return types where matched item code needed bitfield-style collision results rather than simple bools.
what_changed: Implemented previously stubbed item functions in itlucky.c, including setup, animation-state transitions, accessory callbacks, egg/item spawning, lifetime checks, hitlag effect pause/resume hooks, and spawned-state logic.

Implemented previously stubbed Maril item behavior in itmaril.c, including spawned initialization, air/ground motion changes, collision handling, velocity updates, lifetime expiry, effect cleanup, and a corrected use of xDD4_itemVar.maril instead of an unrelated chicorita item-var field.

Implemented large portions of Octarock item behavior in itoctarock.c, supported by expanded Octarock item vars and attributes. The provided context shows this file had the largest churn in the PR, but the excerpt does not include the exact function bodies.

Implemented large portions of Whispy Apple behavior in itwhispyapple.c, supported by a new itWhispyAppleAttributes struct and an added xDD8 field in itWhispyApple_ItemVars. The review notes that Whispy Apple code ANDs the result of it_8026DAA8, motivating a non-bool return type.

Added common item attribute/item-var layouts for itWhispyAppleAttributes, itMarilAttributes, itMaril_ItemVars, itLuckyAttributes, itLucky_ItemVars, expanded itOctarock_ItemVars, and itOctarockAttributes in itCommonItems.h.
Changed it_8026D5CC from void to bool and returned mpColl_8004B108(coll) after it_80276214(item_gobj), updating the declaration in it_266F.h.
Changed it_8026DAA8 from bool to s32 in it_266F.c/.h, stored mpColl_800471F8 in an s32, tested it against zero, and ORed in it_80276308(gobj), preserving collision-result bits.

Replaced several UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations in item headers with typed prototypes such as bool it_802D5124(Item_GObj*), void it_802D51C8(HSD_GObj*), void it_802D5420(HSD_GObj*), void it_802D5560(HSD_GObj*), and Item_GObj* it_802D5710(Item_GObj*, Vec3*, Vec3*, f32).

Added needed includes for matched implementations, including m2c_macros.h, ef/eflib.h, itCommonItems.h, it/items/itegg.h, lb/lb_00B0.h, lb/lbvector.h, sysdolphin/baselib/random.h, and math.h where required by the new code.
smart_moves: Inferred that collision helper it_8026DAA8 could not be bool because caller code, specifically Whispy Apple per review, bitwise-ANDs the return value; changing it to s32 preserved collision flag bits and made downstream matches possible.
Propagated function signature fixes into headers rather than leaving local casts or UNK prototypes, improving type information for later item decompilation.

Modeled item-specific xDD4_itemVar overlays with named per-item structs, allowing matched code to access Lucky, Maril, Whispy Apple, and Octarock state through semantically grouped fields instead of raw offsets.

Used small static inline helpers in matched functions where needed to shape codegen, for example in Lucky hitlag/accessory setup and spawn construction paths, instead of forcing all logic into one function.

Preserved matching artifacts explicitly when still unresolved, such as PAD_STACK calls and an M2C_FIELD(attr, s32*, 4) TODO in it_2725_Logic44_Spawned, making uncertainty visible instead of over-renaming or inventing a field.
Removed unnecessary function-pointer casts where the corrected prototype already matched, e.g. calls to it_8027A118(gobj, it_802D5420) and it_8027A118(gobj, it_802D69E4).

Used existing engine helpers consistently in item state changes, including Item_80268E5C for motion transitions, Item_8026AE84 for sound/effect triggers, efLib_PauseAll/ResumeAll for hitlag hooks, and item collision helpers from it_266F.
decomp_lessons: Do not assume collision wrappers return bool just because they are used in conditions; if callers combine results with bitwise operations, use an integer/bitfield type such as s32 and propagate that type outward.

Matched item behavior often requires first defining the per-item item-var and special-attribute layouts in itCommonItems.h; getting those overlays close enough unlocks readable source in item .c files.

Header cleanup is part of matching work: replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with concrete Item_GObj, HSD_GObj, Vec3, f32, bool, and Item_GObj* signatures reduces casts and makes later callers easier to match.
When an attribute offset is known only from codegen but not yet structurally named, retaining M2C_FIELD with a TODO is preferable to guessing a misleading field name.
Bitfield unions can be useful for item-var flags, as shown by itMaril_ItemVars.x60 containing individual bit members and an aggregate flags view.
Per-item state fields should be accessed through the correct item-var union member; the Maril physics fix from xDD4_itemVar.chicorita.x64 to xDD4_itemVar.maril.x64 is a cautionary example.
Leaving PAD_STACK markers in newly matched functions can be necessary to preserve stack layout while other parts of the function or surrounding types remain imperfect.
naming_conventions: Item attribute structs follow the pattern it<ItemName>Attributes, e.g. itWhispyAppleAttributes, itMarilAttributes, itLuckyAttributes, and itOctarockAttributes.
Per-item runtime variable structs follow the pattern it<ItemName>_ItemVars, e.g. itWhispyApple_ItemVars, itMaril_ItemVars, itLucky_ItemVars, and itOctarock_ItemVars.
Unknown fields remain offset-style names such as x0, x4, x10, x18, x60, x64, x68, x6C, xDD8, x2C, and x32 when their semantic purpose is not established.
Comments in structs preserve item-pointer-relative offsets, such as ip+DD4, ip+E34, ip+E38, and ip+E3C for itLucky_ItemVars.
Matched item functions retain address-derived names such as it_802D5050, it_802D5124, it_802D5710, it_802D66F8, and it_802D69E4 when no semantic symbol name is available.
Motion callback names use the existing item motion pattern, such as itLucky_UnkMotion5_Anim, itLucky_UnkMotion6_Phys, itMaril_UnkMotion1_Coll, and itMaril_UnkMotion2_Anim.

Inline codegen helpers are named after the containing function with an _inline suffix, e.g. it_802D533C_inline, it_802D546C_inline, it_802D5648_inline, it_802D5710_inline, and itMaril_UnkMotion1_Coll_inline.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed function return types to match register/value usage: it_8026D5CC now returns the result of mpColl_8004B108, and it_8026DAA8 returns an s32 collision-result bitfield.
Used PAD_STACK with explicit byte counts in several functions to preserve stack shape during matching.
Used M2C_FIELD for an unresolved Lucky attribute access while still matching the function body.
Introduced static inline helpers to coax matching code structure for repeated setup blocks and spawn initialization.
Kept explicit comparisons such as result == false and test != 0 where they likely reflected required codegen patterns.
Used bitwise OR accumulation for collision results in Maril and shared collision code, e.g. coll = it_8026D9A0(gobj); coll |= it_80276308(gobj); coll |= it_802763E0(gobj).
Preserved concrete float operations and constants visible in the excerpt, including random velocity generation for Lucky and M_PI-based Maril rotation timing.
Removed casts to callback function types after correcting callback prototypes, reducing artificial codegen workarounds.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.comment: Reviewer noted that bool had seemed plausible, but the Whispy Apple code ANDs the result, so the function must return some kind of bitfield. They suggested propagating that information up to mpColl_800471F8 and other users eventually, but said it was not necessary for this PR.
review_feedback.outcome: The PR kept it_8026DAA8 as s32 in this slice; broader propagation to mpColl_800471F8 was left as future work.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that propagate the s32/bitfield return type from it_8026DAA8 into mpColl_800471F8 or other collision helper declarations.
Inspect subsequent work on itwhispyapple.c to see whether xDD8 and itWhispyAppleAttributes fields gained semantic names.
Search for later renames of itLuckyAttributes fields x10/x14/x18 and the M2C_FIELD(attr, s32*, 4) TODO in it_2725_Logic44_Spawned.
Compare later Octarock PRs for semantic names for itOctarock_ItemVars.x18, x2C, x32 and itOctarockAttributes fields.
Search for cleanup PRs that remove PAD_STACK or static inline matching helpers introduced in itlucky.c and itmaril.c.
